title: barbarea brachycarpa subsp. anfractuosa
intro: Barbarea brachycarpa subsp. anfractuosa, also known as the upland bittercress, is a type of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is commonly found in North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Barbarea brachycarpa subsp. anfractuosa include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of kidney stones, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Barbarea brachycarpa subsp. anfractuosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
